defs: Very miserable; sunk in, or accompanied by, deep affliction or distress, as from want, anxiety, or grief; calamitous; woeful; very afflicting.
quotes: O cruel! Death! to those you are more kind Than to the wretched mortals left behind.
The wretched refuse of your teeming shore . . .

defs: Worthless; paltry; very poor or mean; miserable; as, a wretched poem; a wretched cabin.

quotes: Nero reigned after this Claudius, of all men wretchedest, ready to all manner [of] vices.
